description: No Reply-To header is present on an otherwise clean, trusted, all-passing message. replyToDomains is empty and replyToDomainMatchesFromDomain is null, so the consistency comparison is skipped and no replyTo.domainMismatch signal is emitted. Confirms a missing Reply-To stays silent rather than producing a noisy low-confidence signal.

authentication-results: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

authenticationResults.raw: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com
